component.name: Krema
component.summary: A lightweight dock for KDE Plasma 6 — spiritual successor to Latte Dock

description.p: Krema is a lightweight dock for KDE Plasma 6, designed as a spiritual successor to Latte Dock. It provides quick access to your favorite applications with smooth parabolic zoom animations, window previews via PipeWire, and deep integration with KDE Plasma desktop features.

Choose from six background styles — transparent, tinted, acrylic frosted glass, Mica (system accent color based), and adaptive opacity that responds to overlapping windows. Full keyboard navigation (Meta+F5) and AT-SPI screen reader support ensure the dock is accessible to all users.
Built from scratch with C++23 and KDE Frameworks 6, Krema is Wayland-native and uses Kirigami for its settings UI, ensuring a fully native KDE Plasma experience.
Fixed icon rendering on HiDPI displays when icon normalization or icon scale is active. Fixed dock edge trigger unreachable in AutoHide/DodgeWindows mode when KDE panel occupies the screen edge.

Icon size normalization with automatic padding detection, projective SDF drop shadow, smooth icon repositioning animation on dock resize, and dock recovery after screen lock and monitor hot-plug. Merged SmartHide into DodgeWindows with DodgeActiveOnly option. Full documentation overhaul with updated feature descriptions and accessibility coverage.
Six background styles (transparent, tinted, acrylic frosted glass, Mica, and adaptive opacity), and comprehensive keyboard accessibility with AT-SPI screen reader support.
Window previews via PipeWire, multi-window preview list, and Settings UI improvements.
